evals.name: E-commerce Order Flowchart
evals.prompt: Draw a flowchart for an e-commerce order process: user places order, stock check, payment, shipping, delivery confirmation. If out of stock, show notification. If payment fails, prompt retry.
evals.expected_output: Well-structured drawio XML with start/end nodes (green), process steps (blue), decision diamonds (yellow), error paths (red). Vertical main flow, branches go right. No overlapping nodes. All edges use orthogonalEdgeStyle.

evals.name: Microservice Architecture
evals.prompt: Draw a microservice architecture diagram with: Nginx gateway, User Service, Order Service, Payment Service, MySQL databases, Redis cache. Show service-to-service calls and data layer connections.
evals.expected_output: Layer-based architecture diagram. Client (red) at top, gateway (green) layer, services (blue) layer, data (blue cylinders + yellow cache) at bottom. Each layer horizontally centered. Dashed lines for cache access.

evals.name: User Registration Sequence Diagram
evals.prompt: Draw a UML sequence diagram for user registration. Participants: User (actor), Frontend, User Service, Database. Show the full registration flow including validation and response.
evals.expected_output: UML sequence diagram with actor + object participants at top, dashed lifelines, activation bars, solid arrows for requests and dashed arrows for responses. Participants spaced 200px apart.

evals.name: Blog System ER Diagram
evals.prompt: Draw an ER diagram for a blog system with: users table (id, username, email), articles table (id, title, content, author_id FK), comments table (id, content, article_id FK, user_id FK). Show relationships between tables.
evals.expected_output: ER diagram using swimlane tables with proper PK/FK notation, divider lines, and ERmandOne/ERmany relationship arrows. Tables spaced in a row or grid with no overlap.

evals.name: AI Agent Mindmap
evals.prompt: Draw a mindmap with 'AI Agent' as the center. Branches: Core Capabilities (Perception, Reasoning, Action), Applications (Customer Service, Programming, Research), Tech Stack (LLM, RAG, Tool Calling), Challenges (Hallucination, Safety, Cost).
evals.expected_output: Radial mindmap with center ellipse (accent blue), 4 branches in different colors spread in 4 directions, leaves fanning out from each branch. Curved edges (no arrows). No overlapping between branches.

evals.name: Home Network Topology
evals.prompt: Draw a home network topology: modem/ONT, router, switch, NAS, smart TV, phone (wireless), laptop. Show wired connections as solid lines and wireless as dashed lines.
evals.expected_output: Hierarchical network diagram. Modem at top, router below, switch below that, end devices at bottom. Solid lines for wired, dashed for wireless. Devices use rounded rectangles with bold labels.

evals.name: UML Class Diagram
evals.prompt: Draw a UML class diagram with: User class (id, name, email; login(), register()), Admin class inheriting from User (with manage() method), Order class (id, total; create(), cancel()) associated with User 1:N.
evals.expected_output: UML class diagram with 3-section boxes (name, attributes, methods). Inheritance arrow (empty triangle) from Admin to User. Association arrow with 1..* label from User to Order. HTML-formatted content inside boxes.

evals.name: Cross-platform Export
evals.prompt: Draw a simple flowchart (start -> process -> end) and export it as PNG.
evals.expected_output: Valid drawio XML saved to .drawio file. Platform-appropriate CLI command used for export (macOS: /Applications/draw.io.app/..., Windows: draw.io.exe from Program Files or LOCALAPPDATA, Linux: drawio). Uses --scale 2 for crisp output.

evals.name: Multiline Label Encoding
evals.prompt: Draw a layered architecture diagram with layer labels split into two lines, such as 'Scene Layer' and 'Application Layer'.
evals.expected_output:
Any forced line break inside XML value attributes is encoded as 
 rather than literal \n, so draw.io renders actual new lines instead of backslash-n text.

evals.name: Balanced Alignment And Dense Fill
evals.prompt: Draw a layered architecture diagram with a right-side cross-cutting concerns sidebar and several grouped modules in the core layer.
evals.expected_output: Sibling boxes are evenly aligned with consistent gaps, incomplete rows are centered, and containers/sidebar are tightened or redistributed so there is no obvious one-sided blank space or oversized unused area.
